patches_submitted.subject: [PATCH] btrfs: always drop root->inodes lock in find_first_inode_to_shrink()

version_history.subject: [PATCH] btrfs: always drop root->inodes lock in find_first_inode_to_shrink()

patches_submitted.patch_summary: This patch modifies the find_first_inode_to_shrink() function in the Btrfs file system to always drop the root->inodes lock before attempting to share the cpu/lock with cond_resched_lock(). This is done by replacing cond_resched_lock() with an open-coded variant that unconditionally unlocks and then locks around a call to cond_resched. The patch addresses a problem where callers of btrfs_del_inode_from_root(), such as kswapd, would spin on xa_lock(&root->inodes) for up to 2.8ms due to the shared lock mechanism. By decoupling lock sharing from cpu sharing, the patch ensures that all users of the lock share it fairly and reduces memory pressure caused by the extent map shrinker.
